fact: October 14 is exactly 287 days into the year, which means there are only 78 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 14, making the days shrink faster than a deflating balloon.
Maple trees are pumping sugar down to their roots like crazy right now, which is why their leaves turn those blazing red and orange colors.
October 14 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, when the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were nearly equal two weeks ago!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 14 is prime baby animal season - lambs, calves, and joeys are being born as spring heats up!
The moon phase around October 14 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called a Hunter's Moon - the second full moon of autumn.
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and some of the ones passing by today have never made this journey before but somehow know exactly where to go!
In northern regions, the first frost can hit around this time, turning spider webs into glittering crystal art that disappears as soon as the sun touches them.
October 14 is roughly 6 weeks after the autumn equinox and 10 weeks before the winter solstice - it's like the middle child of fall!
Bears in cold climates are eating up to 20,000 calories per day right now - that's like eating 40 hamburgers every single day to get ready for winter sleep!
